Template web application to be used as a starter kit for future projects and a training tool to teach responsive web design to other developers.  

NOTE:  All graphics in this project are not my property and were definitely not used with permission.  They serve as placeholders only, to demonstrate regions, and are not re-purposed or resold.  This project should be used for training purposes or to quickly get started with responsive HTML5.  If cloning, please replace all graphics marked as "replace with your own..."

YELLOW TAKES THE STAGE

This 1960's bathroom had original lemon-yellow sinks that needed to be replaced - unless, of course, they became a focal point.
The sinks were in surprisingly good shape. So, rather than replacing them, I saved some money by replacing the faucets only, refinishing the cabinetry, and focusing on the yellow instead of trying to hide it.

Drupal 7 website designed for knowledge sharing, articles, and documentation on technical topics.  Content filtering based on role.  Site is internal to the organization (not available on the web).

Highlights: Integration to MS Active Directory though the LDAP module.

I created this Bridesmaid's necklace for a wedding with an interesting color combination: red and turquoise. 

The pendant is real turquoise mineral, and the beads are stone, glass and sterling silver.

This bedroom had a problem that many older homes suffer from: paneling.  Old, brown, plastic-like paneling and matching trim.  Ideally, one would remove paneling and replace it with drywall, but in a pinch, it can also be painted.  Painting both the paneling and trim in a warm white color saved time and money, and gave the room a naturally "beachy" feel.  So, continuing on that line, I added accessories in green and blue.  I used seashell stencils and chalk paint to create coordinating wall hangings with some old picture frames I painted white.  The only purchases in this room were the twin bed and linens.

title: Family Room and Home Office Combo

body.value:
This extra-large room boasts five windows, a outside entry, and a floor-to-ceiling natural stone fireplace.  It spans the width of the house, and on the back side, it gets only indirect morning sun.  With the help of mature trees and neighboring houses, what would be hot afternoon sun on the western side is filtered enough to make this room a cool and serene space.  Because of its size, the room is multi-functional as both a family room and a home office.  It's also incredibly difficult to photograph without professional help (as you'll see from the photos).  

The fireplace paired with original oak flooring feels cold and hard by itself but is softened by some greenery, lighter-colored decor and an oversized sectional in the adjacent corner.  

The home office is simple, with an understated desk and single, black shelf.

field_short_description.value: Family Room and Home Office Combo

body.value: This sunflower was inspired by an image I saw online.  I'm not a fantastic painter, but I'm quite happy with the result.
This rooster was created in a paint-nite style workshop with an instructor.  I've been to tons of instructor-led painting classes, and this was, by far, my favorite.
Old clay flowerpots got a makeover with some tile mortar and seashells.

Even though I'm a shower person, and I've often considered gutting this bathroom and removing the oversized jet tub, it's cheaper to simply decorate the tub area with peaceful, relaxing accessories.  

The WASH, SOAK, and RELAX signs were inspired by elaborately staged bathrooms on Pinterest, but mine were made with wine corks, some old cedar planks I found in the basement, and a glue gun.  The vintage ceramic pitcher was a yard sale find, and the bronze-y accessories I've collected over the years and decided to bring them together in this space.

I love shades of green and grey together, so this is one of my favorite pieces.  It has a toggle clasp closure with a cool square pendant.  The beads are glass, acrylic, and stone with sterling silver accents.

This bracelet was created with multiple strands of grey leather attached to findings with wrapped silver wire.  Each strand has a different pattern of grommet, tube, and sterling silver beads.  The extra-large lobster clasp makes it easy to put on and take off with one hand.

Stretch bracelets are super comfortable and easy to make.  In fact, some of my first jewelry creations were simple stretch bracelets like these.

For this project, I was inspired by the Kinsley Armelle collection and decided to try my own version.  I used charcoal-colored stone bead with gold-plated accents, and I stole open-work tube beads from some broken Silpada bracelets to complete the look.

The most time-consuming and complex piece I've created so far, this necklace is fit for a black tie affair.

Pearls in multiple sizes and shapes, Czech glass, and dainty sterling silver chain come together with wire-wrapping hidden under special, oversized end caps.  It closes at the back with a sterling silver toggle clasp.

Woven bracelets tend to be very intricate and give the impression of a high level of difficulty.  But with the right pattern and instruction (and a bit of patience at the beginning), they are very doable.

I created this bracelet in an instructor-led class held at a local bead store.  It did take some time - about 2 hours - to complete, but the end result is a bracelet that gets a lot of attention and compliments.  I've since made several other bracelets in different colors using the same pattern. 

The beads are two shades of olive green with gold seed bead accents and a brass closure.

Live Drupal 8 application.  Custom Bootstrap-based administrative theme.  Site building and design, custom module development, Views Plugin development, Twig theming, feed imports and integration to other systems.

Highlights:  Fully-customized administrative theme, 2000+ user accounts, advanced Views development, custom entities, jQuery and Javascript front-end scripting.  This solution replaced 40 separate Java applications and streamlined the Time Off request process for this healthcare provider by simplifying and standardizing an overly-complex app.

body.value:
Inspired by some artwork I saw on Pinterest, I thought, "That's cool!  I can do that myself!"  

It was not a quick project, however.  This painting is LARGE.  It's about 6 feet wide by 4 feet tall.  Purchasing a painting of this size – or even purchasing a blank canvas of this size – was cost prohibitive.  So I made the canvas myself with a roll of the canvas material, 1x2 boards, and a lot of staples.  

The canvas isn't perfect.  It warped a little once I began painting, and the frame soaked up paint and liquid.  But I'm very happy with the results.  It hangs in my home office and provides a nice backdrop for Zoom meetings.

One thing to note, I used the same paint color I put on the walls, but mixed it with water, white, black and other shades of green to create the different "sections" within the painting.  That way it matches perfectly with my dark green wall and other office decor.

field_image.alt: abstract painting in shades of green
